reviews.review_msg: Great prof! Though this class was blended (online/in person). I had few struggles with the materials and assignments given. If you have the chance definitely take a class with Andy!

The course was blended as lecture were online and tutorial was in person. Dr. Heard was very accommodating during the term for my circumstances. His response time was fast. The lectures were informative, engaging and interesting. Assignments and exams were fair. I highly recommend.

Very personable, clearly passionate about students and politics. Releases crosswords which are a godsend to study from. Workload is heavy, attend every lecture, start essays EARLY as he does not guide you on it. Exams a little unfair as they require loads of extra reading. My favourite class out of the semester. Great guy!

Andy is a tremendous professor. He is enthusiastic about the material and makes learning politics a delight. He is approachable and knowledgeable with plenty of experience. He explains tests and papers thoroughly and makes lectures interesting and enjoyable. Would definitely recommend.

One of my fav poli sci profs. His class is not that much of a workload but keeping up with the readings/lectures is necessary as it will be a lot to cram for if you do not. I found his marking to be fair and he was always willing to help students if they needed help.

One of my favourite POL classes so far. Gives great additional feedback on assessments when you request it. You must work hard for your grade but I believe Prof Heard is a fair grader over all. Assessments are lecture and textbook based. Overall I really enjoyed the material in this course as well as the professor.

Andrew was an amazing professor. He's very caring about his students and want everyone to succeed in his class. His exams are very heavily based on lectures and readings. I would take another class with Andrew again.

Professor Heard was amazing! He was very kind, approachable, and easy going, but also provided good feedback and fair grading. He provided really great insight to class topics and kept the course interesting. I would love to be taught by him again!
One of the best profs at SFU. Caring and tries to make the class as interesting as he can. Posts the lectures online as well so you can re watch before the midterm or final.

He reminds me of Tigger. He is so friendly and makes learning about constitutional law very entertaining and easy to understand, which is impressive given the complexity of Canadian federalism and constitutional interpretation. Graded fairly, recorded lectures, but messed up both our midterm and final, so that was slightly frustrating.
